prompt:
undo.ts drives undo/redo in the diagram editor and users are furious that undoing a typed sentence takes one keystroke per undo. Two features, both spec'd in test_coalesce_checkpoint.ts:

Coalescing. execute() gets an optional second arg { coalesceKey }. Consecutive executes with the SAME key extend the entry on top of the stack instead of pushing — one undo() reverts the whole run (reverts in reverse order, obviously), one redo() replays it, and the entry keeps the FIRST command's label in historyLabels(). The run is broken by: a different key, a keyless execute, any undo/redo, or a checkpoint. After a break, the same key starts a fresh entry — I've seen editors merge a new word into an entry from before an undo and it's data loss, don't.

Named checkpoints. checkpoint(name) marks the current stack position (re-declaring a name moves it), checkpoints() lists the names. revertTo(name) undoes step by step back to the mark, returns how many steps it took (0 if you're already there), and leaves those steps redoable. Unknown name -> throw mentioning it's unknown; a checkpoint you've undone past is gone -> throw mentioning 'unreachable'.

test_undo.ts covers the shipped behavior and stays green; neither test file gets edited. node --test test_undo.ts test_coalesce_checkpoint.ts
